heading: Deployment tools built for real sites
description: Five flagship tools we deploy most often — each matched to environment, budget, and compliance needs.

tools.title: Drone Scanning
tools.summary: A modern, sophisticated approach to inventory and asset tracking — drones fitted with bar code or RFID scanners fly warehouse aisles and auto-sync scan data to your inventory system.
tools.description: Drones fly within warehouse aisles and scan tags; scan data is automatically transferred from drone memory to your inventory management system. Flights can be pre-timed at night or scheduled in dedicated slots. Best suited for environments where inventory is stacked properly.
tools.image_alt: Drone scanning inventory in a warehouse
highlights: Reduce material resources and their occupancy rate
Enrich inventory tasks and optimize human resources
Automatically integrate data into your inventory management system

tools.title: Robotic Scanning
tools.summary: Ideal for retail — autonomous robots scan inventory and assets across outlets, plants, warehouses, and offices with advanced shelf and stock intelligence.
tools.description: Robotic scanners move through retail and industrial environments, reading tags and delivering operational insights beyond a simple stock count. Built for organizations that need continuous, repeatable verification at scale.
tools.image_alt: Robotic scanning in a retail environment
highlights: Identifying products kept out of place

tools.title: RFID Fixed Readers
tools.summary: Installed at fixed entry, exit, and bay points — reads RFID tags on inventory and assets passing within proximity, with no line-of-sight scanning required.
tools.description: Suitable for factories, warehouses, and corporate offices. All items passing within approximately 10 metres can be read via this reader — a strong fit for high-volume inventory and asset movement zones.
tools.image_alt: RFID fixed reader at a warehouse entry point
highlights: Reading with no line-of-sight or item-by-item scans required
Multiple tags can be read at a time
Technology automates data collection and vastly reduces human effort and error

tools.title: RFID Handheld Readers
tools.summary: The most common tool for stock and asset verification — with read range up to 10 metres and clear advantages over conventional bar code readers.
tools.description: Typically offering read range up to 10 metres, RFID handheld readers score over conventional bar code readers in terms of read range, read rate, and automated reconciliations.
tools.image_alt: RFID handheld reader for asset verification
highlights: Higher read rate than conventional bar code readers
Auto reconciliation between books and physical stock
Most widely deployed tool for field verification

how_it_works.text: Asset or inventory information is uploaded in the scanner
Tags are scanned by the handheld reader
Upon scanning, asset or inventory records are auto-reconciled between book record and physical reading

tools.title: Mobile Tag Scanning
tools.summary: Lightweight mobile applications to scan bar code and QR code tags — linked to your stock or asset management application for instant reconciliation reports.
tools.description: A cost-effective entry point for organizations that need quick deployment, low reader investment, and semi-automated tracking workflows.
highlights: Short, excess, and reconciled reports generated automatically
